steps.m: A-L-M (the letter names Alif, Lam, Meem)
steps.n: These are special letters that appear at the start of some chapters of the Quran. They are read by saying each letter's name separately, like saying 'A-B-C' in English. Their exact meaning is known only to Allah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
Changed vowels to show this happened to someone rather than someone doing it

steps.m: she/it was defeated (referring to الرُّومُ - the Roman Empire)
steps.n: Added تِ to show this refers to a feminine subject (the Roman Empire)
This is a proper noun referring to the Roman/Byzantine Empire and their people
ٱل at the start makes it specific/definite, like 'the' in English. The hamza is written as ٱ because it's connecting with the next word.

steps.m: the Romans (subject of غُلِبَتِ - were defeated)
steps.n: The ّ (shaddah) emphasizes the ر and the ُ (damma) shows this is the subject of the verb غُلِبَتِ (were defeated)

steps.m: in (location in أَدْنَى ٱلْأَرْضِ - lowest land)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment. In this ayah, it shows location within the 'lowest land'.

steps.m: in (location in أَدْنَى ٱلْأَرْضِ - lowest land)
steps.n: The ى with ٓ (alif) is an alternate spelling of ي that doesn't change the meaning
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
Added أَ at the beginning and changed the end to ى to make it mean 'nearest/lowest'. This form is used to compare things or show something is the most of a quality.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together
Added ال to make it specific - this is the actual earth being referred to in the ayah

steps.m: of the earth (after فِي in أَدْنَى)
steps.n: The kasra ending shows this word follows the preposition فِي (in) and connects to أَدْنَى (lowest part)
Added damma to show this is a pronoun meaning 'they'
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to something
Added shadda because the next word begins with ب, causing the ن to merge with it in pronunciation
Added small ن mark (tanween) to help connect smoothly with the next word بَعْدِ

steps.m: to be far away from something

steps.n: Connected the letters and added vowel marks to make it a word meaning 'after' - just like how being 'far' in distance became 'after' in time
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to the word that comes after it (their defeat)
Added هِمْ at the end to show the defeat belongs to them
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
Added سَ at the beginning to show this will happen in the future
This is a basic preposition that shows location or time. In this ayah, it connects to بِضْعِ سِنِينَ (a few years) to show when something will happen.

steps.m: to cut into parts, or a small number

steps.n: Added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'a few' (specifically 3-9). This is a special word in Arabic that means a number between 3 and 9.
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word فِى (in/within). When a word comes after فِى in Arabic, we add kasra at the end.
Root meaning 'passing time' becomes the word for 'one year' by adding special vowels
Changes vowels to kasra (ِ ) and adds ين to make it plural
Adds fatha at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the word بِضْعِ (meaning 'few')
This is the unique name of God in Arabic. It's related to the root meaning of divinity but has become a proper noun.

Added لِ at the beginning to show this belongs to or is for Allah. The ِ at the end shows this word is connected to what comes after it. In this ayah, it shows Allah owns or controls الْأَمْرُ (the matter/affair).
Added vowel marks to turn the command action into a noun meaning the command itself or the matter being commanded

steps.m: the matter/affair (subject of this part of the ayah)
steps.n: Added ٱلْ to make it specific and ُ to show it's the main subject being talked about in this part of the verse

steps.m: from (connecting قَبْلُ 'before' to what precedes it)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects the word قَبْلُ (before) to show the starting point of time being discussed.

steps.m: to be in front of something

steps.n: Changes from verb to noun meaning 'before'
Added ُ at the end to show this word is working as the main subject in this part of the sentence. It pairs with مِن to mean 'from before'.
This is a basic particle meaning 'from' that shows where something starts or comes from
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it

steps.m: to be far away from something

steps.n: Connected the root letters together with fatha and sukun. This word comes after the word مِن (from/after) in this ayah.
Added ُ at the end to show this word is doing something in the sentence, not being acted upon
Added fatha at the end because this word is being used as a time expression (meaning 'on the day')
When ئِذٍ is added to يَوْمَ (day), it means 'on that specific day' - like pointing to a particular day
Conjunction وَ connects it to previous phrase
Added vowels to connect the letters and show past tense form

steps.m: he becomes happy (subject of يَوْمَئِذٍ - on that day)
steps.n: Changed to present tense. The يَ at the start shows it's happening now and means 'he'. The ُ at the end shows it's a normal statement (not a command or wish).

steps.m: to feel/be safe, secure, or trusted

steps.n: Changes from verb (to feel safe) to noun (believer) using common doer pattern
Adding definite article makes the noun definite
Sound masculine plural form indicates multiple believers
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed from 'he helped' (action) to just 'help' (thing) by removing vowels
Added kasra at the end because this word is possessed by Allah (اللَّهِ) in the phrase 'victory of Allah'
Added the preposition بِ to show this victory is the means by which something happens
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'

This is the unique name for the one true God in Islam. The ال makes it definite (like 'the'), the shadda emphasizes the ل, and the kasra at the end shows it belongs to or connects with something else in the sentence.
Basic past tense verb form with fatha vowels
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This word describes who does the action in يَشَآءُ (he wills).
This is a special word that means 'whoever' or 'the one who'. In this ayah, it connects to the verb يَنصُرُ (helps/supports), showing who Allah helps - meaning 'whoever He wills'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
The weak letter ي changes to ا, which is a common change in Arabic verbs
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at the start and ُ at the end
Added long alif to show ongoing/extended wanting
This is a pronoun that means 'he' and refers back to Allah who is the one giving victory

steps.m: and He (subject of يَنصُرُ - helps/gives victory)
steps.n: The prefix وَ means 'and'. This connects this description of Allah to what comes after (that He is the Mighty, the Merciful)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Adding ي in the middle and changing vowels makes this describe someone/something that has this quality very strongly

steps.m: The Mighty One (a name/attribute of Allah)
steps.n: Adding ٱلْ makes it specific and ُ shows it's the subject. This is one of Allah's names describing His perfect might and power.
Changed from basic root meaning 'mercy' to فَعِيل pattern meaning 'one who shows intense/constant mercy'
Adds the nominative case ending (like adding 'the' in English)
Changed the root letters from separate to connected form and added vowel marks to make it a noun meaning 'promise' (this is the object of يُخْلِفُ - 'breaks' in this ayah)
Added fatha at the end to show this is what's being acted upon - Allah's promise is what won't be broken
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the ل, making this a unique name for The One God
Added kasra at the end to show this connects to the word before it (وَعْدَ meaning 'promise'). Together they mean 'promise of Allah'.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يُخْلِفُ (break/fail) into 'does not break/fail'. It's like adding 'not' before a verb in English.
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation

steps.m: to break/fail to fulfill (especially a promise)
he breaks/fails to fulfill (the promise وَعْدَهُۥ)
steps.n: Transforms root into present tense verb form. This word connects to وَعْدَهُۥ (His promise) in the ayah, showing what is being broken/unfulfilled.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
This is the unique name for the One True God in Islam. The ال makes it specific, and the doubled ل shows the specialness of this name. The ُ shows this is the doer in the sentence.
Changed the root into a noun by adding vowel marks. This word is part of a phrase 'promise of Allah' (وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ)
Adding fatha at the end shows this word is receiving the action of the verb يُخْلِفُ (breaks/fails)
Adding هُۥ at the end shows the promise belongs to Him (Allah)
This is the basic form of the word that shows contrast with what came before
Added و at the beginning to connect this contrasting idea with the previous statement

steps.m: and yet truly (connecting to لَا يَعْلَمُونَ - they do not know)
steps.n: Added نَّ at the end to add emphasis, showing this is a strong contrast with what came before. This word introduces the phrase 'they do not know' to contrast with Allah's promise mentioned earlier.
Added vowels (fatha and damma) to make it mean 'it became many' in the past
Added أَ at beginning and changed middle vowel to sukun to make comparative form. This word describes 'the people' in this ayah, saying 'most of the people'.
Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'social beings' which became the common word for people
Added ال to specify these particular people and doubled the ن with shadda

steps.m: of the people (possessed by أَكْثَرَ - most)
steps.n: Added kasra to show this word belongs to أَكْثَرَ (most) in the phrase 'most of the people'
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it mean the opposite. In this ayah, it makes يُخْلِفُ (breaks/violates) mean 'does not break/violate'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, putting sukun on ع, and ُ at the end

steps.m: they know (referring to most people in لٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ)
steps.n: Added ون to show multiple people (they). This refers back to 'most people' (أَكْثَرَ ٱلنَّاسِ) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding ي at the start shows this is happening now

steps.m: they know (referring to the disbelievers mentioned in context)
steps.n: Adding ون changes subject from 'he' to 'they'
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changes from an action to describing someone/something that has that quality
The ًا ending shows this word describes how they know something, like saying 'They know it visibly' in English
This is a preposition that shows where something starts or comes from
The م gets doubled (shadda) because it comes right after a word ending with ن (يَعْلَمُونَ) - this is a common rule in Arabic pronunciation
The fatha at the end shows this connects to the next word ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ (the life)

steps.m: to live, be alive, have life

steps.n: Added و between the root letters to create a noun form. The و replaces one of the ي letters to make pronunciation easier.
Added ة and lengthened the vowel with ٰ to create the noun form indicating the state of living
Added ٱلْ to make the noun definite. The hamza is written as ٱ because it's at the start of the word.
Added kasra at the end because this word follows مِّنَ (from) in the ayah

steps.m: worldly life (because it's closer to us than the afterlife)
steps.n: Changed the root letters to make a special feminine word that means 'closer/nearer thing', which came to mean 'this world' since it's closer to us than the afterlife

steps.m: the worldly life (contrasted with ٱلْءَاخِرَةِ - the afterlife)
steps.n: Added 'the' at the beginning. The ل combines with د to make it stronger (shown by ّ). This word is being contrasted with 'the afterlife' (ٱلْءَاخِرَةِ) in this verse.
Added fatha to show this is a connecting word. This connects what comes after it to the previous statement about 'they know'.
Added هُ which is a pronoun meaning 'he' or 'it'
Added م with sukun to change the pronoun from singular 'he' to plural 'they'. This word is the subject of غٰفِلُونَ (heedless) in this ayah.
This is a basic preposition that shows distance or separation from something
The kasra (ِ) is added at the end to connect this word smoothly to the next word ٱلْءَاخِرَةِ (the Hereafter)

steps.m: to come after, to be later

steps.n: Root letters أخر became a noun meaning 'last' by adding a long 'aa' sound at the start
Added ة to make it feminine and turn it into a specific noun meaning 'the hereafter'

steps.m: the hereafter (contrasted with ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا - the worldly life)
steps.n: Added 'the' (ال) at the start and a kasra (ِ) at the end because it comes after عَنِ (about)
This is a pronoun meaning 'they' (for a group of males or mixed group). In this ayah, it's the subject of غٰفِلُونَ (heedless/unaware).

steps.m: to ignore or be unaware of something

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changes from a verb to a noun describing someone who does this action
Makes the word plural to refer to multiple people who are unaware

steps.m: those who are unaware/heedless (plural, in a grammatical state)
steps.n: Adds the case ending to show the word's role in the sentence
This particle turns a statement into a question, like adding a question mark in English
Adding وَ connects this question to the previous statement

steps.m: And did they not (think about يَتَفَكَّرُوا۟)
steps.n: Adding لَمْ makes the question negative and puts it in past tense. This word introduces a question about whether people have thought about something, connecting to the verb يَتَفَكَّرُوا۟ (think/reflect) that follows.
The basic meaning involves deep contemplation
Adding vowel marks to create the basic past tense verb form
Shadda intensifies the meaning of contemplation
تَ prefix shows doing the action to/for oneself - thinking deeply about something within oneself
يَ at start shows ongoing reflection. The word relates to أَنفُسِهِم (themselves) which is what they're reflecting about.
وا changes it to mean 'they' (multiple people) instead of 'he'. They are reflecting about أَنفُسِهِم (themselves).
Basic preposition that shows location or containment. Here it connects the verb 'think' (يَتَفَكَّرُوا) with what they should think about - 'themselves' (أَنفُسِهِم).
The ي is written as ى with a small alif (ٓ) above it when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing convention and doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to breathe, be precious, or relate to the self
steps.n: Basic root meaning relates to breath/spirit/self
Root letters got vowels to make the common word for 'self/soul'
Changes from singular 'self' to plural 'selves' by adding أَ at the start and changing the last vowel - this is a common plural pattern

steps.m: themselves (referring to the people being discussed)
steps.n: Added هِم to show possession/belonging. This word appears in a question about whether people have reflected within themselves.
Basic particle form meaning 'what' or 'that which'. In this ayah, it introduces what Allah created (the heavens and earth).
Added a shadda (doubling mark) to connect smoothly with the previous word. This word shows what was created.

steps.m: to create or make something from nothing
steps.n: Basic meaning of creation

steps.m: he created (subject of the verb that created السَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ - the heavens and earth)
steps.n: Added fathas (َ) after each letter to show this happened in the past. This word shows that Allah did the creating of the heavens and earth.
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the basic noun form meaning 'a deity'

steps.m: Allah (the one true God) (subject of خَلَقَ)
steps.n: Changed to the unique name Allah, which comes from combining ال (the) with إله (deity) and making special changes to show this is the one true God. This word is the subject (doer) of خَلَقَ (created) in this ayah.

steps.m: to rise up or be elevated
steps.n: Root related to height and elevation
Added vowels to make a basic past tense verb
The verb 'to rise up' became the noun 'sky' by adding hamza at the end
Changed sky/heaven into plural by adding وات at the end
Added definite article and doubled the س (sun letter rule)

steps.m: of the heavens (object of خَلَقَ - created)
steps.n: Changed ending to kasra (i-sound) to show this is what Allah created. Small spelling change in middle of word.
Connected the root letters together with basic vowel marks to form the noun
Added the definite article 'the'
Added 'and' to connect with previous word السَّمٰوٰتِ (the heavens)

steps.m: and the earth (object of خَلَقَ - created)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object that Allah created, connecting to the verb خَلَقَ (created) earlier in the verse
This is a basic particle that means 'what' or 'that which'. It's used to ask questions or refer to things in general.
Added و with fatha at the beginning to mean 'and'. This connects this phrase to what came before it in the ayah, linking to the word خَلَقَ (created).

steps.m: to be separate or clear from something else
steps.n: Root related to separation/between
Adding vowels changes it from root to usable word meaning 'between'

steps.m: between them both (referring to السَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ)
steps.n: Added هُمَا at the end to show this means 'between them both', referring to the heavens and earth mentioned in the ayah

steps.m: between them both (referring to السَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ)
steps.n: Added elongated alif (آ) at the end for emphasis in recitation, but doesn't change the meaning

steps.m: except (connects بِٱلْحَقِّ to what came before)
steps.n: This is a basic particle that shows something is being excluded or is an exception. In this ayah, it connects بِٱلْحَقِّ (with truth) to what was mentioned before.
Added basic vowels to connect the letters
The middle letter is doubled to add emphasis and make it a noun
Add ال to make it specific
Add بِ to show how something is done

steps.m: with/by the truth (in relation to خَلَقَ - created)
steps.n: Added kasra (small diagonal line) under the last letter to connect it with 'created' - shows HOW Allah created everything

steps.m: to set a time limit, to delay until a specific time
steps.n: Root related to time/term
Root letters got vowels (harakat) to make the noun 'time/term', with tanwin at end to show it's indefinite (like 'a/an' in English)
Added و with fatha to connect this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'
Root related to naming
Added مُ at start and changed the ending to show this is something that received a name (like when you name a baby)
Adding shadda on م makes the sound stronger to emphasize this is something that received the action of naming
Added tanween to show this is indefinite (like 'a' or 'an' in English)
Basic emphasis word with kasra under first letter and shadda on final letter. This adds strength to whatever comes after it in the sentence.

steps.m: and indeed (connects to previous statement while emphasizing what follows)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this statement to what came before it in the ayah
Added vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb meaning 'to increase' into an adjective meaning 'a lot of something'

steps.m: many (describing how many people are disbelievers in لَكٰفِرُونَ)
steps.n: The tanween alif (ًا) makes this word describe how many people are being referred to in the phrase 'indeed many among the people are disbelievers'
Basic preposition showing where something comes from or what it's made of
Added shadda to emphasize the meaning since this word comes after another word ending in a similar sound
Added fatha at the end to connect this word to the next word النَّاسِ (the people)

steps.m: to be friendly or social with others
steps.n: Root related to humanity/sociability
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
The words are not directly related - نَاس (people) comes from a different root أ-ن-س meaning 'to be social', not from the verb أَنَسَ (to be friendly). They just look similar.
Added ٱل to make it specific ('the people'), doubled the ن for emphasis, and added kasra at the end because it follows the word مِنَ (from/of) in this ayah
Root related to meeting
Root letters ل-ق-ي turned into noun لِقَاء by adding vowels and alif, changing from 'to meet' into 'a meeting'
Added kasra at end to show possession or connection. Long alif (آ) is used instead of regular alif (ا) for writing style.
Added the preposition بِ at beginning to show how something is done. This word connects to رَبِّهِمْ (their Lord) in the ayah.
Root related to lordship/nurturing

steps.m: lord (one who nurtures and cares for others)
steps.n: Added fatha on first letter and doubled the middle letter to create the noun meaning 'lord'
Added kasra under the last letter to show it's being possessed by something
Added هِمْ at the end to show it belongs to them

steps.m: to reject or cover up truth
steps.n: Root related to disbelief/covering
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed to describe someone who does the action of rejecting truth
Added ون to show multiple people

steps.m: surely they are those who reject truth
steps.n: Added لَ at beginning to add emphasis, showing certainty
This particle turns a statement into a question
Added و to connect this question with what came before it

steps.m: And did they not...? (connects to يَسِيرُوا۟)
steps.n: Added لَمْ which negates the past tense. This introduces the verb يَسِيرُوا۟ (travel/journey) that follows.
Changed from root letters to a noun meaning 'the act of traveling' by adding vowels
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense and changed vowels to make it a verb
Added damma (ُ) at end to show it's a normal statement
Added وا to show multiple people are doing the action
Basic preposition showing location - connects 'travel' (يَسِيرُوا۟) with 'the earth' (ٱلْأَرْضِ) to show where the traveling happens
The ي at the end is written as ى when it's at the end of a word - this is just a writing change that doesn't affect the meaning
Adding short vowels to make the basic noun 'earth/land'
Adding ال makes it definite. This word follows the preposition فِى (in) in the ayah.
The word gets a kasra (small diagonal line) under the last letter because it comes after a word like 'in' or 'of'
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
The ي prefix indicates 3rd person masculine singular in present tense

steps.m: they look/will look carefully (referring to those who travel through the land)
steps.n: The وا shows multiple people are doing the action together

steps.m: so they may look carefully (at what happened to previous nations)
steps.n: The فَ at the beginning shows this looking happens as a result of the traveling mentioned before
This is a question word used to ask about the manner, state, or condition of something
Adding vowel marks to make the word pronounceable - fatha on ك and sukoon on ي
Adding fatha at the end to complete the word
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed weak letter و to ا, which is common in hollow verbs

steps.m: to follow after something or come as its result

steps.n: Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb
Added alif after first letter to show something that results from an action. This word is the subject of كان (was) in this ayah.
Changed alif to alif with horizontal line above (special spelling in Quran). Added damma at end to show this is the doer of the verb كان (was).
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific. The shadda (ّ) appears because ال combines with the first letter.

steps.m: those who (referring to the people mentioned in كَانَ)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to make it plural (more than one). This word refers to the people being discussed in the verb كَانَ (were).
Basic preposition showing starting point or origin. In this ayah, it connects the word قَبْلِ (before) with هِمْ (them) to mean 'before them'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is being possessed by what comes after it

steps.m: before them (referring to previous nations mentioned in ٱلَّذِينَ مِن)
steps.n: Added هِمْ which means 'their/them', referring back to the previous nations mentioned
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form
Changed weak letter و to ا, which is common in hollow verbs
Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action
Added optional pronunciation marks that don't change the meaning
This root carries the basic idea of strength or intensity
Connected the letters and doubled the last د with shadda to show intensity
Added أَ at the beginning to show comparison. This word is comparing the strength of previous nations to those being addressed.
Added sukun (ْ) on ن to show end of first part
Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being referred to (them)
Root meaning 'strong' becomes a noun 'strength' by adding damma (u sound) and doubling middle letter
Added ة at the end to make it a specific instance of strength

steps.m: a strength (as the object being described)
steps.n: Added ً (an) at the end to show this word is receiving the action - it's what is being measured or compared
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: he caused to stir up (acts on الْأَرْضَ)
steps.n: Added أ at beginning to show causing someone/something else to do the action. This verb acts on الْأَرْضَ (the earth) in the ayah.

steps.m: they caused to stir up (acts on الْأَرْضَ)
steps.n: Added وا to show a group of people did the action

steps.m: and they caused to stir up (acts on الْأَرْضَ)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this action with what came before it in the ayah
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and make a basic noun
The ال makes it 'the earth' (specific), and the ending َ shows it's being acted upon

steps.m: to build, develop, or make a place livable

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to show this happened in the past
Added وا to show many people (all male or mixed group) did this action together

steps.m: they developed it (referring to الْأَرْضَ - the earth)
steps.n: Added هَا which means 'it' and refers back to the earth (الْأَرْضَ) they were developing
Added وَ at the start to connect this action with the previous one in the ayah
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this is a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning and changing the middle vowel to sukun (ْ) makes this word mean 'more' of something. Here it's comparing their strength.

steps.n: Adding these vowel marks turns the verb into a noun meaning 'creation' or 'the act of creating'
The ِ at the end shows this word is connected to Allah (الله) in the phrase خَلْقِ ٱللَّهِ meaning 'Allah's creation'
Adding لِ at the beginning shows purpose or direction - 'for' or 'to' the creation
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One True God. The hamza is dropped in this special word.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, shadda on lam, and changed alif to wasl form. This word is possessed by فِطْرَتَ (natural state/disposition) in this ayah.
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show distance
Added ل with kasra (ِ) to show more distance
Added كَ at the end to make it 'that' - a word used for pointing to something far away. Here it points back to فِطْرَتَ (natural state) mentioned earlier.

steps.m: to follow a way or path

steps.n: The kasra and long vowel create a noun meaning a complete system or way of life
The shadda emphasizes that this is a complete system
Adding ال makes it refer to the specific way of life (Islam)

steps.m: the complete way of life (subject of ذٰلِكَ)
steps.n: The damma shows this word is the subject being referred to by ذٰلِكَ (that) in this ayah
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
The و changes to ي with a shadda to show intensity of the quality. This describes someone or something that strongly maintains or establishes something.

steps.m: the one who maintains/establishes firmly (describing الدِّينُ)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it specific. Here it's describing الدِّينُ (the religion) as being firmly established and upright.

steps.m: the firmly established, upright one (describing الدِّينُ)
steps.n: The ُ shows this word is describing الدِّينُ (the religion) in the ayah
Added shadda and kasra to make the word stronger and add emphasis
Added و at the beginning to connect this contrasting statement with what came before it
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added أَ at the beginning to make it mean 'most/majority'. Shows that most of 'the people' (ٱلنَّاسِ) are being talked about.

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others
people (those who are social by nature)
steps.n: Changed from a root meaning 'being social' into a noun meaning 'people' by adding a long 'aa' sound in the middle
Added emphasis by doubling the ن sound
Added ال to point to specific people rather than just any people

steps.m: of the people (owned by أَكْثَرَ - most)
steps.n: Added kasra to show this word belongs to أَكْثَرَ (most) - showing 'most of which people?'
This is a negative particle that negates the verb يَعْلَمُونَ (they know) that comes after it, making the meaning 'they do not know'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added يَ at start and changed vowels to show this happens in the present/future. The ْ (sukun) on ع shows there's no vowel there.

steps.m: they know/are knowing (referring to a group of men or mixed group)
steps.n: Added ونَ at the end to show multiple people (male or mixed group) are doing the action

steps.m: to return or go back repeatedly

steps.n: Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding the vowel 'aa' between first two letters
Added أ at the start to show doing the action for/towards Allah - like turning back to Him in repentance
Changed from past tense 'he returned' to describe someone who keeps returning to Allah. Like changing 'ran' to 'runner'.
Added ين ending to show this describes multiple people
This is a basic preposition showing direction or destination. It's often used with verbs of motion or direction.
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for adding the pronoun. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to Him (referring to Allah in مُنِيبِينَ إِلَيْهِ - turning back to Him)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show this means 'to Him'. In this ayah, it shows the direction of turning back (مُنِيبِينَ) is toward Allah.
Added reflexive pattern to show protecting oneself. The ت is doubled with shadda to show strong emphasis.
Changed to command form for multiple people
Added connecting word 'and'
Added pronoun 'Him' referring to Allah. The alif also gets the wasla mark (ٱ) since it's now in the middle of connected speech.
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Added hamza at beginning to show causative meaning
Changes from past to present tense
Added وا at the end to change from 'I establish' to 'you all establish!' (making it a command for a group)

steps.m: and establish! (command to many people) (connected to وَٱتَّقُوهُ)
steps.n: Added و to connect this command with the previous command in the ayah (وَٱتَّقُوهُ)
Connected the root letters with short vowels (fat-ha) to make a basic word for prayer
Changed from general 'prayer' to mean 'one prayer' by adding ا and ة at the end
Added 'the' (ال) at the start, which combines with ص to make it stronger (doubled)
Changed alif to و with superscript alif for proper noun spelling
Added fatha to show this is the object of the command 'establish' (أَقِيمُوا۟)
This is a negative particle that negates the verb تَكُونُوا۟ (be) that comes after it, changing its meaning to 'do not be'
Added 'and' at the beginning to connect this negative command with the previous commands in the verse (وَٱتَّقُوهُ and وَأَقِيمُوا۟)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
تَ at start shows this is 'she' form in present tense
Added وا to show this applies to a group of people, and the form shows this is a command or wish
The small circle (۟) helps show this is for multiple people
This is a basic preposition showing being part of or coming from a group. In this ayah, it connects to تَكُونُوا (be) to show what not to be among.

steps.m: from/among the (الْمُشْرِكِينَ - those who associate partners with Allah)
steps.n: The َ (fatha) is added because the next word starts with ال. This helps connect the words smoothly when reading.

steps.m: one who joins partners with Allah
steps.n: The مُ prefix and ِ vowel work together to make this mean 'someone who does the action'

steps.m: those who join partners with Allah
steps.n: Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people. This comes after مِنَ (from) in the ayah.
ٱلْ makes it specific - these particular polytheists being talked about
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from
Added fatha at the end to connect smoothly with the next word ٱلَّذِينَ (those who). This is a common change in Arabic to make words flow better when speaking.
Added ال to make it specific and shadda to emphasize the connection
Added ين at the end to make it plural, referring to multiple people. This word describes the people who divided their religion, as mentioned in فَرَّقُوا (they divided).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb
The doubled middle letter shows this action is done with extra force or multiple times
The suffix وا shows a group of people (they) did this action. This word connects to دِينَهُمْ (their religion) which comes after it in the ayah.

steps.m: to follow a path or way

steps.n: Changed the verb into a noun. This word is what is being divided/split up by the verb فَرَّقُوا (they divided).
Added هُمْ to show it belongs to them. The fatha (َ) is added before هُمْ to connect it smoothly. This word shows what they divided - their religion.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The و changes to ا because it's a weak letter in the middle
Adding وا makes it refer to a group of males in the past tense
Adding و connects this word to what came before it

steps.m: to split into groups or follow different paths

steps.n: Added vowel marks to create a plural noun showing different groups that split from each other

steps.m: into sects/groups (object of فَرَّقُوا - they divided)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this word is receiving the action of 'dividing' in the phrase 'they divided their religion into sects'

steps.m: to be complete or surround entirely

steps.n: The doubled middle letter makes the meaning stronger and more complete
The final damma shows this word is the subject of فَرِحُونَ (rejoicing) in this ayah
Added the vowels to form a noun meaning a group of people united for a cause
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this is any party/group (indefinite)
Added kasra (ِ) to show this is a preposition meaning 'with' or 'by'

steps.m: with what/with whatever (connected to فَرِحُونَ - rejoicing)
steps.n: Added مَا which means 'what' or 'whatever'. In this ayah, it connects to فَرِحُونَ (rejoicing), showing what they are rejoicing with/about.
This is a basic preposition that shows something is near or in someone's possession
Added sukun on ي to prepare for connecting with the next part
Added هِمْ at the end to show this refers to 'them'
Added vowels to show this is about feeling an emotion. The pattern فَعِلَ is often used for emotions and temporary states.

steps.m: they are happy (referring to those who divided their religion)
steps.n: Added ون to show this describes multiple people. This word describes the state of those mentioned earlier in the ayah who divided their religion.

steps.m: when (introduces the condition of مَسَّ ٱلنَّاسَ ضُرٌّ - harm touching people)
steps.n: This is a word that means 'when' and introduces a time condition. In this ayah, it introduces what happens when harm touches people.

steps.m: and when (introduces the condition of مَسَّ ٱلنَّاسَ ضُرٌّ - harm touching people)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it in the larger discussion
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
The doubled س with shadda shows this is an intense or severe touching/affecting. This word describes what happened to people (ٱلنَّاسَ) that made them call upon their Lord.

steps.m: to be social, friendly with others
people (those who are social by nature)
steps.n: Changes the basic idea of being social into a word for the beings who are social - humans/people

steps.m: the people (specific group being talked about)
steps.n: Adds ال to show we're talking about specific people, not just any people
Doubles the ن to add emphasis to the word

steps.m: the people (being affected by مَسَّ - touched/afflicted)
steps.n: The fatha shows that the people are being affected by the action مَسَّ (touched/afflicted) in this ayah
Changed the word from an action (harming) into a thing (harm) by adding ُ and combining the last two letters with shadda
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is talking about harm in general, not a specific harm. This word appears in a sentence about when harm touches people.
Added fathas to connect the letters. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end of a past tense verb.

steps.m: they called upon/prayed to (subject of يُشْرِكُونَ)
steps.n: Added the plural marker وا to show multiple people did the action. This word is connected to يُشْرِكُونَ (they associate partners) later in the verse, showing these are the same people who first prayed to Allah but then committed shirk.

steps.m: to care for and help grow

steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who does a lot of caring and helping

steps.m: their Lord (who they call upon in دَعَوْا)
steps.n: Adding هُم shows this is their Lord. This word connects to دَعَوْا (they called) earlier in the verse, showing who they called upon.

steps.m: to turn back or return repeatedly

steps.n: Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding the 'aa' sound in middle
Changed to show someone who keeps returning to Allah repeatedly
Added emphasis by doubling the م

steps.m: those who earnestly return (to Allah in repentance)
steps.n: Added the sound masculine plural ending to show multiple people
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or movement towards something
The ى changes to ي before adding pronouns. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: to him (they turn to him in repentance)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show who is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers to Allah, as people turn to Him when in difficulty.

This is a connecting word that shows one thing happens after another, with some time passing in between. For example, if you say 'I woke up, then I ate breakfast', it means you didn't eat immediately after waking up - there was some time in between.
This is the basic conditional word that means 'when' for likely future events

steps.m: when (with emphasis, indicating a future condition)
steps.n: The alif is elongated (آ) to add emphasis and extend the sound, making the word more prominent in speech
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning makes the action affect someone else - like changing from 'taste' to 'make someone taste'
Adding هُم shows who the action was done to - in this case, 'them'
Basic preposition meaning 'from'. The sukun (ْ) is added to show where the word stops.

The shadda (ّ) on م shows it blends with the sound of a similar letter that came before it in speech
Added vowel marks to connect the letters together and make it a noun

steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a time clause. In this ayah, it connects the time when Allah gives people a taste of mercy to what happens after. It's like saying 'at the moment when' or 'as soon as' in English.

steps.m: to split or separate into distinct parts

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changed the middle vowel to kasra and added ي to create a noun meaning 'something characterized by being separated'

steps.m: a group (subject of يُشْرِكُونَ - they associate partners)
steps.n: Added tanwin (double damma) to show this is the indefinite subject doing the action in the verse
Starting with the basic preposition مِن which means 'from'. Added sukun to show end of word.

Added هُم at the end to show who is being referred to (them).

steps.m: to care for and help grow
master who takes care of others
steps.n: The doubled middle letter shows this is someone who really takes care of others completely
Added بِ at the beginning to show this is about doing something with or by someone
Added هِمْ at the end to show it's their Lord. This word is what they are associating partners with in the verb يُشْرِكُونَ (they associate partners).

steps.m: to make something a partner/equal with something else

steps.n: Adding vowels makes this a past tense verb showing someone made partners with Allah

Added ون to show multiple people doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to people associating partners with Allah after He has shown them mercy.

steps.m: to reject or deny something by covering it up

steps.n: Added vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action

steps.n: Added لِ to show purpose - they do this action as a result of what was given to them
This is a relative pronoun that means 'what' - it introduces a clause describing something

steps.m: with/by what (connects to كَفَرُوا - they disbelieved)
steps.n: The prefix بِ adds the meaning 'with' or 'by'. In this ayah, it shows how or why they disbelieved - because of what was given to them.

Changed from 'coming' to 'giving' by adding ءَا at the start, making it mean 'to bring/give something to someone'. The ى at the end shows it's past tense.
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action
Added هُمْ at the end to show who received the action (them)
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at start and doubled middle letter to show doing the action to yourself with extra effort

Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels

steps.m: any authority/proof (object of أَنزَلْنَا - we sent down)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this is the object being sent down in the ayah
Added vowel marks to clarify pronunciation: ُ (u) on ه and َ (a) on و

steps.m: to speak or communicate with words

The ت at the beginning shows the action is done to or with oneself

steps.m: with what/that which (refers to يُشْرِكُونَ - they associate partners)

Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two fathas
Added وا to show multiple people did this in the past
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'with'
Added ه with kasra to show this refers to 'him/it'. This pronoun refers back to what they associate as partners with Allah in the verb يُشْرِكُونَ (they associate partners).

steps.m: to make something a partner/equal with something else

Added يُ at start and changed vowels to show it's happening in present/future. The ُ on يُ and ِ on ر show it's present tense.
Added ون at the end to show multiple people doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to those who associate partners with Allah in worship.
This is a conditional particle that shows when something happens. It's a basic word that isn't built from a root.

Changed the final alif to a long alif (آ) to show it's being stretched when read. This doesn't change the meaning.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning makes someone cause the action to happen to someone else
Adding نَا at the end shows 'we' did the action. The weak letter و is dropped when نَا is added.

steps.m: to be social or friendly with others

Added ٱل to make it specific and doubled the ن with shadda

steps.m: the people (object of أَذَقْنَا - we gave them to taste)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this word is receiving the action of 'giving taste to' in the verse

steps.m: to be merciful, to show compassion

steps.n: Changed from root letters to noun meaning 'mercy' by adding vowel marks (َ and ْ)
Added ة to make it a specific instance of mercy
Added tanween to show this is something being received by people (object of أَذَقْنَا - we made them taste)
Changed from root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding vowels
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did this action
Small mark above وا to show correct plural pronunciation
Added kasra to show this is a preposition meaning 'with' or 'by'
Added هَا which refers back to the mercy (رَحْمَةً) mentioned earlier in the ayah. This shows what they are happy with.

Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb

Completes the broken plural pattern with the middle ي

steps.m: when (followed by هُمْ يَقْنَطُونَ - they despair)

Independent pronoun for 'they' (referring to people who lose hope when bad things happen). This pronoun is the subject of the verb يَقْنَطُونَ (they lose hope) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to form the past tense verb
Adding يَ at the start and changing vowels shows this happens in the present/future
Adding ون makes it refer to a group of people (masculine plural)

Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'

steps.m: The One True God (subject of يَبْسُطُ - extends)
steps.n: Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One God. The shadda shows emphasis. This word is the subject (doer) of يَبْسُطُ (extends/spreads out) in this ayah.

steps.m: The One True God (object of يَرَوْا - they see)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being seen in the phrase 'Do they not see that Allah...'
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. This word is connected to يَشَآءُ (he wills) in this ayah, showing Allah spreads provision for whom He wills.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changes the verb (providing) into a noun (the thing being provided)

The fatha shows this word is receiving the action of يَبْسُطُ (extends/spreads) in the ayah
Combination of لِ (for) and مَن (who/whoever)

steps.n: Present tense verb showing ongoing action. This verb is paired with يَبْسُطُ in the ayah to show Allah's complete control over provision - both extending and restricting it.

steps.n: Added 'and' at the beginning
Added kasra (ِ) under the alif to show correct pronunciation. This is a primitive particle (not derived from a root) used for emphasis.

steps.m: indeed/verily (emphasizes the statement يَبْسُطُ ٱلرِّزْقَ that follows)
steps.n: Added shadda (ّ) to strengthen the emphasis. This particle introduces and emphasizes the statement that follows about Allah extending provision.
Basic preposition showing location or containment. Since this is a particle, it doesn't come from a root.
Added kasra (ِ) under ف to show correct pronunciation
Changed the final ي to ى (alif maqsurah) which is a common spelling variation at the end of words
The basic pointing word 'this' gets an alif on top (ٰ) to show distance
Added ل with kasra (لِ) to strengthen the meaning of pointing to something far

steps.m: that (pointing to what was just mentioned)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show we're pointing to something specific that was just talked about - in this case, how Allah extends or restricts provision

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be apparent

steps.n: Root letters were shaped into a noun pattern that means 'one clear sign or proof'
